news.title:
Brokerages highlight stock picks like BPCL, GAIL, Tata Steel, SBI Life, and Ambuja Cement across energy, cement, and insurance sectors.
Analysts favor 360One, Nuvama Wealth, Max Healthcare, and domestic cyclicals, citing resilience, market recovery potential, and strategic positioning.

news.title:
Max Healthcare approves acquisition of controlling stake in Kalinga Hospital.
No journalist-authored analysis or commentary provided in the announcement.
news.summary:
Max Healthcare approves acquisition of controlling stake in Kalinga Hospital.
No journalist-authored analysis or commentary provided in the announcement.

news.title:
Max Healthcare to acquire 58.4% stake in Kalinga Hospital for ₹300 Cr, expanding its footprint in Eastern India.
Board approves ₹100 Cr loan for Kalinga Hospital's revamp and ₹300 Cr external borrowing for acquisition.
news.summary:
Max Healthcare to acquire 58.4% stake in Kalinga Hospital for ₹300 Cr, expanding its footprint in Eastern India.
Board approves ₹100 Cr loan for Kalinga Hospital's revamp and ₹300 Cr external borrowing for acquisition.

news.title:
Max Healthcare approves buying 58.39% stake in Kalinga Hospital for ₹300 crore.
Additional financing includes ₹100 crore loans and $5M via ECBS.
news.summary:
Max Healthcare approves buying 58.39% stake in Kalinga Hospital for ₹300 crore.
Additional financing includes ₹100 crore loans and $5M via ECBS.

news.title:
Max Healthcare reappoints director for 3 years from May 2026.
Acquires 58.4% stake in Bhubaneswar hospital for INR 300 Cr.
news.summary:
Max Healthcare reappoints director for 3 years from May 2026.
Acquires 58.4% stake in Bhubaneswar hospital for INR 300 Cr.

Growth driven by 43% capacity addition, CGHS occupancy, and hospital ramp-ups.

news.title:

Expansion plans on track; no Delhi-NCR capacity concerns; open to selective acquisitions.

news.title:
Kotak Equities updates its model portfolio, adding Groww and Swiggy, while removing Max Healthcare and Pidilite Industries.
Groww is favored for its capital market presence, while Swiggy faces concerns over competition and food delivery sustainability.
news.summary:
Kotak Equities updates its model portfolio, adding Groww and Swiggy, while removing Max Healthcare and Pidilite Industries.

news.title:
Sensex and Nifty opened higher amid diplomacy hopes; Sensex at 79,501.32 (+0.49%), Nifty at 24,607.95 (+0.52%) by 9:45 am.
Coal India (+2.83%), ONGC (+2.67%), Reliance (+2.33%) led gains; Max Healthcare (-1.32%), HCL Tech (-0.98%) faced declines.
news.summary:

news.title:
IT stocks lead recovery post Anthropic-triggered selloff; Nifty reclaims 25,500, Sensex gains 485.2 points to trade at 82,711.1.
Infosys, HCLTECH, TCS, TECHM top Nifty gainers; Dr. Reddy's, MAXHEALTH, SBI among losers; FIIs net sellers at ₹102.5 crore.
news.summary:

news.title:
Max Healthcare's GST demand reduced from ₹2.59B tax and ₹2.54B penalty to ₹85.4M each.
Management intends to appeal further despite partial relief granted.
news.summary:
Max Healthcare's GST demand reduced from ₹2.59B tax and ₹2.54B penalty to ₹85.4M each.

news.title:
Max Healthcare plans to expand capacity by over 8,900 beds.
Approximately 4,600 beds will be added in the next 3-4 years.

news.title:
Max Healthcare shared an updated Feb 23 presentation covering growth drivers, financials, and strategy.
The presentation highlights healthcare facilities, capacity, technology, and research with cautionary notes on forward-looking statements.

news.title:
Max Healthcare Institute Ltd (Symbol: MAXHEALTH, NSE; Scrip Code: 543220, BSE) disclosed an investor presentation for the 17th Kotak Chasing Growth Conference scheduled on Feb 23, 2026, at Grand Hyatt, Mumbai.
The presentation includes updates on Max Healthcare's operations, financials, and growth strategies, and will be available on the company's website, www.maxhealthcare.in.
news.summary:
Max Healthcare Institute Ltd (Symbol: MAXHEALTH, NSE; Scrip Code: 543220, BSE) disclosed an investor presentation for the 17th Kotak Chasing Growth Conference scheduled on Feb 23, 2026, at Grand Hyatt, Mumbai.
The presentation includes updates on Max Healthcare's operations, financials, and growth strategies, and will be available on the company's website, www.maxhealthcare.in.
